homepage.heroSubline: Transform your space with tailored designs built for Chicago living. From historic bungalows to modern condos, we craft kitchens that endure our harsh climate.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Onyx Kitchen Remodel grew from a single carpenter's vision into Chicago's trusted remodeling team. Our roots in Lincoln Park inform every project with authentic local craftsmanship.
We blend Chicago's architectural heritage with contemporary functionality. Every layout considers how real Cook County families cook, gather, and weather our extreme seasons.
When we're not installing custom cabinetry, you'll find us supporting neighborhood schools and sponsoring Logan Square's annual home tour.

valueProps.title: Chicago-Tested Materials
valueProps.description: We specify finishes that withstand lake-effect humidity and freeze-thaw cycles without sacrificing style.

homepage.ctaSubline: Schedule a free design consultation with our Chicago-based team today.

services.name: Custom Cabinetry
services.shortDescription: Handcrafted storage solutions designed for Chicago's unique homes.
services.description:
Chicago's historic architecture demands cabinetry that fits like it was always there. Our craftsmen build inset doors for Lincoln Park brownstones and space-saving pull-outs for Lakeview condos with precision joinery.

We use Midwest-sourced maple and walnut finished with moisture-resistant coatings to combat lake-effect humidity. Every design includes Chicago-specific touches—like deeper uppers for bulky winter cookware or integrated heating vents for drafty bungalows.

From Hyde Park's professor homes needing library-style pantries to Schaumburg's busy families requiring kid-friendly storage, we tailor each configuration to how you actually live.
process: Step 1: In-home assessment of existing space and structural limitations
Step 2: 3D rendering showing traffic flow and appliance access
Step 3: Wood selection from our local mill partners
Step 4: Professional installation with dust-containment systems
benefits: Framing adapted for Chicago's brick-and-plaster walls
Soft-close hardware rated for -20°F winters
Adjustable shelving for seasonal storage needs
Ventilation-compatible designs for older homes

faq.question: How do you handle uneven walls in old Chicago homes?
faq.answer: We scribe cabinets to fit irregular surfaces and use shim systems developed specifically for the area's settling foundations. No gap fillers needed.

faq.question: Can you match my historic trim details?
faq.answer: Our workshop maintains profiles for common Chicago molding patterns from 1890-1940. We'll replicate your home's original character.

services.name: Quartz Countertops
services.shortDescription: Durable surfaces engineered for Chicago's extreme temperature swings.
services.description:
Chicago's freeze-thaw cycles wreak havoc on natural stone, but our quartz installations withstand the abuse while elevating your kitchen's aesthetic. We source slabs with Chicago's climate in mind—prioritizing brands with thermal shock resistance for homes near Lake Michigan.

For downtown high-rises, we offer lightweight options that meet condo board requirements. In Arlington Heights' ranch homes, we design waterfall edges that modernize without clashing with midcentury lines. Every template session includes heat-map analysis to prevent warping near radiators.

Our sealed seams and under-mount sink installations prevent the moisture damage common in Naperville's humid summers. Choose from finishes that complement Chicago's design legacy—from steel-look slabs for industrial lofts to creamy whites for suburban light-filled spaces.
process: Step 1: Digital templating with laser measurement tools
Step 2: Full-sample selection at our West Loop showroom
Step 3: Precision cutting with dust-free onsite fabrication
Step 4: Seam placement strategy for thermal expansion
benefits: Stain resistance against Chicago's deep-dish pizza nights
Non-porous surface prevents bacterial growth in humid months
Reinforced edges for heavy holiday cooking loads
UV-stable colors for sunlit breakfast nooks

faq.question: Will quartz crack during Chicago winters?
faq.answer: Our installation includes expansion joints and thermally stable adhesives specifically formulated for the area's 70°F annual temperature swings.

faq.question: How do you protect floors during installation?
faq.answer: We use stair-climber dollies and temporary floor protection systems developed for Evanston's narrow historic staircases.

services.name: Smart Kitchen Upgrades
services.shortDescription: Technology integrations that respect Chicago's architectural heritage.
services.description:
Modern convenience shouldn't mean sacrificing your home's character. Our smart kitchen solutions blend seamlessly into Chicago's diverse housing stock—from hidden charging drawers in Victorian-era cabinets to under-counter refrigeration in Lincoln Square's galley kitchens.

We prioritize locally relevant tech: WiFi-enabled leak detectors for old plumbing systems, motion-activated task lighting for dark winter evenings, and appliance garage systems that conceal mixers in open-concept lofts. For Aurora's multigenerational households, we install discreet safety features like automatic stove shut-offs.

Every system undergoes cold-weather testing at our Bridgeport workshop. You'll get voice-controlled lighting that works through January's blizzards and touchless faucets with freeze protection for unheated mudrooms. We even program your devices with Chicago-specific commands—try "Hey Google, preheat the oven for deep dish."
process: Step 1: Home network assessment and compatibility check
Step 2: Concealed wiring plans preserving original moldings
Step 3: Smart device selection based on household habits
Step 4: Personalized training with troubleshooting manual
benefits: Discreet tech that doesn't clash with historic details
Whole-home integration for Chicago's interconnected living
Backup power options for frequent outages
Child-lock systems rated for Illinois safety codes

faq.question: Will smart devices work in my brick bungalow?
faq.answer: We install WiFi extenders designed for thick masonry walls and place hubs in central locations—often repurposing original built-ins for seamless tech integration.

faq.question: How do you protect electronics from winter condensation?
faq.answer: Our proprietary vapor barriers and strategic venting prevent moisture damage—a system perfected through years of servicing lakeside properties.

services.name: Historic Kitchen Restoration
services.shortDescription: Preserving Chicago's architectural legacy one kitchen at a time.
services.description:
Evanston's Arts and Crafts homes and Logan Square's worker cottages deserve kitchens that honor their past while meeting contemporary needs. Our restoration specialists combine archival research with modern craftsmanship to recreate period-appropriate details—from hexagonal tile patterns found in 1920s bungalows to authentic milk paint finishes.

We salvage original elements whenever possible, carefully restoring Chicago's distinctive features: built-in iceboxes converted to wine storage, clawfoot tubs repurposed as farmhouse sinks, and salvaged lumber milled into new countertops. For landmark-designated properties, we navigate permit processes with city preservation boards.

Every project begins with historical research at the Chicago Architecture Center. You'll receive documentation of your home's original layout alongside our sensitive modernization proposals. We even replicate vintage hardware using local foundries—because your 1910s kitchen should age gracefully for the next century.
process: Step 1: Architectural history consultation and salvage assessment
Step 2: Materials sourcing from local architectural salvage yards
Step 3: Craft reproduction elements using period techniques
Step 4: Final finishes hand-applied by our restoration specialists
benefits: Tax credit documentation for historic districts
Salvaged materials from Chicagoland's architectural gems
Traditional joinery techniques for authentic repairs
Future-proof updates hidden behind original surfaces

faq.question: Can you match my home's original stained glass?
faq.answer: Our partnership with Chicago Art Glass allows us to reproduce intricate patterns using the same kilns and techniques as early 20th century artisans.

faq.question: How do you update plumbing without damaging historic walls?
faq.answer: We use endoscopic cameras to map pipe routes and employ trenchless technology developed for Chicago's landmark buildings.

services.name: Universal Design Kitchens
services.shortDescription: Accessible layouts for Chicago's aging housing stock.
services.description:
Chicago's iconic two-flats and postwar ranches often present mobility challenges. Our universal design approach reimagines these spaces for comfortable aging-in-place—without compromising style. We lower countertops in Arlington Heights' midcentury kitchens using clever graduated heights that maintain clean lines. For Wicker Park's narrow row houses, we install space-saving appliances with accessible controls.

Every design considers Chicago's specific needs: heated flooring for arthritic joints during winter, task lighting positioned for seasonal affective disorder, and slip-resistant tile that stands up to salted boots. We even create custom solutions like retractable pot racks for wheelchair users in bungalows with limited cabinet space.

Our certified aging-in-place specialists navigate Illinois building codes while preserving your home's character. The result? A kitchen that adapts to life's changes—whether that means accommodating grandchildren in Naperville or recovering from knee surgery in a Gold Coast high-rise.
process: Step 1: Mobility assessment and future needs consultation
Step 2: Ergonomic layout planning with physical therapists
Step 3: Safety feature integration preserving aesthetics
Step 4: Adjustable systems for evolving requirements
benefits: Chicago-specific solutions for narrow stairwells
Multi-height work surfaces for diverse households
ADA-compliant designs that feel residential
Non-slip surfaces tested on icy entryways

faq.question: Can universal design work in my compact condo?
faq.answer: We've perfected space-saving solutions for Chicago's smaller units, like toe-kick drawers and appliances with collapsible handles that meet accessibility standards.

faq.question: How do you make vintage kitchens wheelchair accessible?
faq.answer: Our patented doorway widening techniques preserve original trim while creating necessary clearance—a method featured in Chicago Landmarks Commission case studies.

reviews.name: Marjorie K.
reviews.text: The team salvaged the original 1923 built-in pantry in our Logan Square two-flat and converted it to a functional spice kitchen. Their tile specialist matched the hexagonal border perfectly using fragments we found behind the walls. Five winters later, everything still looks immaculate.

reviews.name: Andre S.
reviews.text: Our Gold Coast condo's new motion-activated under-cabinet lighting has been life-changing during dark winter mornings. The installers hid all wiring in the original moldings—you'd never know it wasn't part of the 1930s building. Even the doorman commented on how tasteful the tech upgrades are.

reviews.name: Linh T.
reviews.text: After two failed installations from other companies, Onyx finally gave us countertops that don't show condensation rings in our humid Naperville summers. They suggested a matte finish that complements our midcentury cabinets and included a hidden hot pad drawer for winter cookware storage.

reviews.name: David R.
reviews.text: My mother can now safely use her Evanston kitchen after Onyx's redesign. The pull-down shelving system means she doesn't strain reaching, and the heated floor prevents slips. Most importantly, it still looks like her historic home—not a clinical space.

reviews.name: Sophia M.
reviews.text: The custom banquette seating with hidden storage has transformed our Lincoln Park kitchen. It accommodates holiday crowds but tucks away neatly for daily use. The craftsmen even incorporated ventilation slots to prevent moisture buildup against our exterior brick wall.

reviews.name: Carlos V.
reviews.text: Our Aurora multigenerational home needed tech that worked for everyone. Onyx installed voice-controlled lighting with Spanish and English commands, plus a stove that automatically turns off if left unattended. The kids love the charging drawer disguised as an old flour bin.

service-areas.title: Chicago Suburbs Kitchen Remodeling | Onyx Service Areas
service-areas.description: Serving Naperville, Evanston, Arlington Heights and beyond with kitchen designs tailored to each suburb's unique housing styles.

serviceAreas.content: Onyx Kitchen Remodel specializes in transforming kitchens in Naperville's upscale family homes, many built between the 1970s and 2000s. Our expertise includes modernizing outdated layouts, upgrading appliances for energy efficiency, and installing durable materials that withstand the area's harsh winters. Whether you're looking for a sleek contemporary design or a timeless traditional aesthetic, we tailor our remodels to fit Naperville's sophisticated suburban lifestyle. Our team understands the unique needs of Naperville homeowners, ensuring your kitchen is both beautiful and functional for years to come.
serviceAreas.localContext: Naperville features upscale family homes from the 1970s-2000s and experiences harsh winters with heavy snow and ice.

serviceAreas.content: Serving Aurora's diverse housing stock, Onyx Kitchen Remodel brings top-tier craftsmanship to kitchens in homes ranging from the 1960s to the 2000s. We specialize in adapting older layouts to modern lifestyles, incorporating weather-resistant materials to handle Aurora's cold winters. From cozy bungalows to spacious suburban homes, we create kitchens that blend functionality with style. Our local knowledge ensures your remodel enhances your home's value while meeting Aurora's unique climate challenges.
serviceAreas.localContext: Aurora boasts diverse housing from the 1960s-2000s and faces harsh winters with heavy snow and ice.

serviceAreas.content: Onyx Kitchen Remodel excels at updating kitchens in Schaumburg's 1970s-80s suburban developments, where many homes are ready for a modern refresh. We focus on optimizing space in these often-compact layouts, using durable materials that stand up to the area's snowy winters. Whether you're preparing for resale or simply want a more functional kitchen, our designs balance practicality and elegance. Schaumburg homeowners trust us to deliver remodels that align with the community's suburban charm.
